compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The auto-lander is a long-running service; it must deploy like the review-bot, log to its OWN service logs (never `session_log`), and — critically — fail LOUDLY and recover crash-safely, since a single-instance bot that wedges silently would reintroduce the manual toil this epic removes.

## What
Containerize the loop service (S2) and wire it into docker-compose (single instance) on the existing Gerrit box; provision its credential + Gerrit capability; make hangs/errors/stuck-states highly visible; and guarantee crash-safe restart. Decomposed into child tasks so each is loadable whole.

## Dependencies
Depends on **S2** (`perovskite-frilly-grebe`, 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec) — the core loop this story packages and deploys. Uses **S3**'s (`darksome-starry-sable`, 0525-6d71-8969-4512) outcome marker for drain/restart reconciliation.

## Design context (shared contracts the children implement)
- **Status model (prior art: Chromium CQ + Zuul/Tide):** per-change outcome is reported BACK onto the Gerrit change (comment + `Autosubmit` label state) which `land` reads via Gerrit; bot liveness + queue-status are served by a read-only HTTP status endpoint exposing **`heartbeat_age_s`** (the field `land`'s `lander_down` binds to).
- **Heartbeat contract (design-fixed):** a state-volume timestamp file, written once per the bot's 15 s poll; freshness ≤ 15 s; `lander_down` at age > 90 s.
- **Reliability posture:** single-writer (flock) + heartbeat + autoheal restart + loud error metric + stuck-state alarm + crash-safe recovery record. HA (external state store) is deferred to a future epic.
- **Stuck-state threshold (design-fixed):** the time-in-phase alarm fires when a `wipChain` sits in any non-`idle` phase for **> 45 min** (the 30-min CI-await bound + margin), configurable — published as the `autolander_time_in_phase_seconds` CloudWatch metric.
- **Autoheal socket:** the autoheal sidecar mounts the Docker socket **read-write** (it must issue `docker restart`); this socket-write privilege is the accepted, inherent cost of autoheal.

## Scope
Deploy + credentials + observability + liveness/recovery. NOT the core rebase/submit loop logic (S2); the heartbeat write, status endpoint, sentinel check, and drain are thin service-lifecycle hooks into `infra/autolander/loop.py` that this deploy story owns.

## Acceptance Criteria (story-level; children carry the implementation detail)
- [ ] **Package + single-instance** — child **S5a** (`08e1-5505-d898-4192`): Dockerfile + compose service + autoheal sidecar + flock guard + external state volume (+ `compose-up.sh SITE_SUBDIRS`).
- [ ] **Credentials + capability** — child **S5b** (`ea61-01c3-daea-4824`): SSM token (no IAM change), `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN`, and the bot's `Rebase` access right on the rebar project's `refs/heads/*` (submit already covered by Contributors membership).
- [ ] **Observability + status endpoint (LOUD failures)** — child **S5c** (`9a6f-065d-91c4-4e03`): `autolander_errors` (three feeders + alarm), the stuck-state time-in-phase alarm, the named `autolander_time_to_land_seconds` metric, and the read-only status endpoint.
- [ ] **Liveness + crash-safe recovery** — child **S5d** (`38be-a778-43f5-4a4e`): HEALTHCHECK + autoheal, heartbeat, emergency-stop sentinel, drain-on-SIGTERM with a concrete recovery record, and the crash-recovery/idempotency test.
- [ ] The service NEVER writes `session_log`; all failure modes (hang, error, stuck, `lander_down`) are visible via metric/alarm for troubleshooting.

compiled_state.title: S5 Deploy (container + compose, single instance) + observability (service logs, not session_log)
